attacking_style: Attacks from breakaways with 1500-2000m to go on climbs or exploits descents tactically when rivals expect recovery. Prefers long-range moves from reduced groups rather than explosive final-km accelerations.
team_tactics: Functions as dual threat: either protected GC option when climbing strongly, or opportunistic stage hunter from breakaways. Team allows flexible role-switching mid-race based on form and situation.
terrain_preference: Medium-length climbs (5-15km) where diesel climbing strength allows bridging or sustained attacks. Vulnerable on explosive short climbs and time trials; descending attacks show tactical intelligence to compensate for lack of pure acceleration.
vulnerability: Final 2km accelerations on steep gradients (11%+) where punchy climbers drop him. Time trialing clearly limits GC ceiling. Positioning errors in echelons/technical sections risk GC losses on non-climbing stages.
key_patterns: Wins from breakaways with perfectly-timed long attacks (1500-2000m), not final sprints—4 breakaway wins observed.
Outsprints GC climbers after hard stages but loses head-to-head sprints against punchy climbers consistently.
Strongest in week 1-2 of Grand Tours; maintains top-5 GC but lacks TT/explosiveness for podiums.
Exploits descents tactically when climbing acceleration insufficient—Romandie 2023 descent attack exemplifies pattern.
Opportunistic bonus-second hunting even after crashes shows time-gaining awareness beyond stage wins.
Diesel climbing allows bridging peloton-to-leaders solo but cannot sustain final acceleration when caught.
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Tour breakthrough (stage win, 5th GC) after four-year drought shows matured tactics and sustained three-week form improvement
